mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing cycle that lowers discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as market participants anticipate lower borrowing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 70%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in payroll growth below 100k new jobs signals weak labor market dynamics that constrain corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reassess earnings outlooks.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed +1.5% in 60%+ of instances when PAYEMS growth falls below 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in consumer spending (CPIAUCSL) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate weakening demand conditions that depress corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risks materialize.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive in 50%+ of periods when both CPIAUCSL and HOUST decline by >1.5% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in industrial production (INDPRO) above 110 while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains below 2% signals strong supply-side dynamics that boost corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of instances when INDPRO exceeds 110 and CPIAUCSL remains below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day moving average crossover of FEDFUNDS above 2.5% signals a tightening cycle that increases equity risk premiums, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as capital flows shift to fixed income.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed +1.0% in 50%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS remains above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) indicates deteriorating consumer purchasing power that pressures equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances when RSAFS declines and UNRATE rises by >0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 4% signals tight labor market conditions that drive wage inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors fear central bank intervention.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed +1.2% in 50%+ of periods when UNRATE falls below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 while GDP (GDPC1) grows below 2% indicates weak supply-side expansion that fails to support equity earnings, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns exceed +0.8% in 40%+ of instances when INDPRO > 105 and GDPC1 < 2% YoY.
